notes: It crashed a few times in the tutorial and Santa Monica loading screens. Ran perfectly afterwards without any tweak. The process won't finish after quitting the game, though.
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes: The game works as well as on windows out of the box... That is, it's buggy :D See in-depth details on the github issue: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/1804
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes:
Okay, this one is a bit more complicated.

This game doesn't work well out-of-the-box even on Windows and that's why the awesome community made "Unofficial Patch" exists which I used for this game. It is highly recommended that you do so as well as otherwise the came might soft-lock on you fairly late in the story and that's the end. Not something you want, trust 2004 me, he was very frustrated.

Find the unofficial patch here: https://www.patches-scrolls.de/patch/4647/7

You can install this patch by running it in regular Wine and simply selecting the games install location. Don't hesitate to also install the + marked changes which alter the game more drastically compared to its original release. Even if you have never played it, it will only help to restore missing content, fix confusing things and make the game much better.

Running on Linux might introduce some additional issues, I need the following launch parameters:

WINEDLLOVERRIDES="dbghelp=n,b" %command% -game Unofficial_Patch

If the door in the beginning of the tutorial doesn't open, try approaching it crouched a couple of times.
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes: This is a bit of a buggy mess even on Windows so I tend to prefer running the GOG version on straight up WINE with the unofficial patch when it comes to this game.
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes: Install the Unofficial Patch and use the launcher that comes with it (the bash script) to play it, the Steam  version is broken.
